name: Esther Phillips
abstract: Esther Phillips (born Esther Mae Washington; December 23, 1935 – August 7, 1984) was an American singer, best known for her R&B vocals. She rose to prominence in 1950, scoring several major R&B hits including "Double Crossing Blues" and "Mistrustin' Blues" under the moniker "Little Esther." In the 1960s, she achieved chart success with t…

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Jazz Cabaret with Cab Calloway

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Griffin
about_quotes.evidence: Esther Phillips is mentioned only as the artist for whom arrangements were written ('Body Talk... Esther Phillips'), with no direct interaction described between Dick Griffin and Esther Phillips.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Griffin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 12, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Purdie
about_quotes.evidence: Well it sounds like the original was done by Little Esther. I did the original. Little Esther Phillips.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bernard Purdie interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Chris Parker
about_quotes.evidence: I remember recording at Rudy Van Gelder’s, a CTI recording with Esther Phillips
about_quotes.doc_title: Chris Parker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 5, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: I remember I did the date... Esther Phillips also is from my home town.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
